title: Once a rising star, Nancy Mace suffers resounding defeat in governor’s race
description: The South Carolina congresswoman finished fifth in the state’s GOP gubernatorial primary, according to unofficial returns, solidly losing even her own home county and district.

title: ICE denies having a protester database. But a letter to Congress sheds more light
description: In a previously unpublicized letter to Congress, the newly-departed head of ICE said the agency collects data on people suspected of potentially unlawful activity, which could include protesters.

title: San Francisco immigration court shuts down, striking at heart of historic advocacy
description: The main San Francisco court was one of the busiest in the country, hearing thousands of cases a year. It was also one of the courts most likely to grant an immigrants' asylum application.

title: Bill Gates to appear before Congress over Epstein involvement
description: Gates will sit for a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

title: House Republicans pass bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol
description: House Republicans have passed roughly $70 billion in funding for immigration enforcement. The package funds ICE and Border Patrol through the remainder of President Trump's term.

title: Rep. James Walkinshaw on funding for ICE and the future of immigration enforcement
description: Congress has passed funding for ICE and Border Patrol through the rest of President Trump's second term. NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Rep. James Walkinshaw of Virginia.

title: In Nevada, Trump's policies are making things tough for Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo
description: In one of the most competitive races for governor this year, Nevada Democratic Attorney General Aaron Ford will challenge Republican Gov. Joe Lomardo for his office in November.

title: House passes $70 billion immigration enforcement funding through Trump’s term
description: The legislation, which brings to an end a four-month lapse in funding, now goes to President Trump to sign into law.

title: Evette, backed by Trump, and Wilson, a Trump supporter, head to S.C. governor runoff
description: In South Carolina, both Republican candidates for governor are MAGA devotees, but Trump only endorsed Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette, not Attorney General Alan Wilson. The two are headed to a GOP runoff.

title: Trump rails at Gov. Moore over pace of Jack Nicklaus golf course project
description: The president and the Maryland governor are at odds again — this time over renovations to golf courses at Joint Base Andrews.

title: House approves labor-friendly bill with support from 20 Republicans
description: The House has approved a bill to slash the time it takes for newly unionized workers to get a first contract. The measure allows for government intervention if a deal is not reached within 90 days.

title: Former Biden official Deb Haaland wins New Mexico primary for governor
description: Haaland could make history as the first female Native American governor if she’s elected.

title: Hilton, Becerra lead in California’s governor primary. See live results here.
description: Latest California governor primary results as Democrats Katie Porter, Tom Steyer and Xavier Becerra and Republicans Chad Bianco, Steve Hilton vie for nominations to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom.

title: House passes bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the remainder of Trump's term
description: The bill provides roughly $70 billion for immigration enforcement and highlights a GOP caucus continuing to endorse Trump's immigration agenda as Democrats warn Congress has ceded its oversight role.

title: Trump has been trying to secure a better nuclear deal with Iran than Obama. Can he?
description: For years, President Trump slammed former President Obama's nuclear deal with Iran. Now he has to push through his own.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Trump’s grip on the GOP leaves Netanyahu with few places to turn
description: As Trump clamps down on Netanyahu, the Israeli leader is finding he has few avenues of appeal among American politicians.

title: Charlie Kirk’s killing convinced Vances to try for a fourth child, JD Vance says
description: The vice president writes that he had urged his wife, Usha Vance, to have another child for years, but she had said she was “done.”
